title: Vercel Production Promotion Tasks
principles: A clean encrypted-variable Preview redeploy is a hard gate before any Production target changes.
Preview and Production use separate proxy URLs, bearer tokens, storage namespaces, profile subjects, OAuth profiles, and cipher keys.
Production deployment requires explicit operator approval after the Preview evidence is accepted.
Keep browser OAuth and plaintext token material in the trusted-local command boundary; Vercel exposes no OAuth start or callback route.
Use canonical Effect Schema contracts, tagged errors, Context services, Layers, Config, Redacted, HttpClient, Command, and KeyValueStore boundaries.

Helper sprawl is forbidden: every new function, module, service, mapper, adapter, and wrapper needs multiple concrete call sites or ownership of a real provider, security, serialization, resource, or policy boundary.
This SPEC adds no React or visible operator route; any frontend requires a separate SPEC following primitive -> composite -> layout -> route and leaf-owned state/commands.
Never print, commit, snapshot, or persist prompt content or secret values in deployment evidence.
globalVerification: Effect language-service diagnostics are reviewed for every changed TypeScript file with no unresolved actionable hints
No React/component/visible route files change; otherwise stop for a separate frontend SPEC and Browser verification
No broad lint suppressions, structural casts, generic helper modules, pass-through services, or dead exports
Sanitized Vercel project, target, deployment, env-name, HTTP, session-stream, runtime-log, storage, and leak-scan evidence

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.description: Before accepting each task, record separate parent audits for ownership/call graph, Effect implementation quality and helper admission, and verification/evidence coverage. Add passes while any production safety, auth, storage, rollback, or attempted frontend-composition finding remains.

tasks.title: Redeploy and prove a clean encrypted-variable Preview
tasks.description: Redeploy proxy then agent from one clean committed source revision using only target-scoped encrypted Vercel Preview variables, and record the complete hosted Eve-to-Codex proof.
implementationNotes: Begin with a clean worktree, pushed source SHA, personal Vercel scope, and read-only verification of required Preview variable names/targets without values.
Deploy the proxy without --env, --build-env, shell-prefixed secret values, pulled env files as build inputs, or prebuilt output carrying local values.

Run the existing sanitized proxy proof against the immutable READY Preview URL and confirm live mode, private auth rejection, authenticated SSE, encrypted readback, refresh/fencing behavior, and clean logs.
Update the encrypted agent Preview proxy URL to that immutable accepted deployment, then deploy the agent with no inline values.
Inspect build logs for Turbo strict-environment warnings and prove turbo.json exposed all Eve build-time model inputs.
Use fresh Vercel project OIDC auth for /eve/v1/info, create one minimal session, replay startIndex=0 through session.waiting, and correlate one authenticated proxy 200.
Record deployment ids, immutable URLs, source SHA, target, model id, event names/counts, statuses, and timings only.
mandatoryVerification: No Production target is changed by this task.
No inline secret/build-env flag or stale local/prebuilt output participates in either deployment.
Flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn proof programs with tagged errors handled in .pipe(...) through catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema-derived evidence/config contracts; no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, JSON.stringify/JSON.parse, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Proxy health/auth/SSE/encryption/refresh/fencing/leak proof and agent info/session/stream/proxy-correlation proof pass against the new deployments.
Targeted app/package checks and bun run verification pass.
completionCriteria: One source SHA has accepted immutable proxy and agent Preview deployments built only from encrypted Vercel variables.
The hosted session proves codex-proxy mode and one correlated live proxy request without secret markers.
Sanitized evidence and all three audit passes are recorded in an active execution plan and this ledger.

tasks.title: Implement the Production preflight and auth/config matrix
tasks.description: Create a read-only, schema-backed preflight that validates Production project ownership, target-scoped config names, auth policy, storage binding presence, stable proxy URL requirements, and rollback inputs without exposing values.
implementationNotes: Keep agent preflight code in apps/agent and proxy/storage/profile preflight code in apps/codex-proxy; reuse @bundjil/codex-oauth contracts instead of copying profile/config DTOs.
Decode the minimal Vercel CLI/API response through Effect Schema at the executable edge; do not mirror full provider responses.

Commit apps/agent/agent/channels/eve.ts with the installed Eve 0.20.0 explicit auth policy eveChannel({ auth: [vercelOidc(), localDev()] }); test that Vercel OIDC is accepted, non-local anonymous callers are rejected, and localDev does not admit Production traffic.

Reject Tilt Legal scope, Preview/branch proxy URLs, shared Preview key prefixes, missing Production-only credentials, mock/local proxy mode, unprotected private routes, and absent rollback deployment ids.
Document the route auth matrix: Deployment Protection, Eve vercelOidc(), proxy bearer auth, health exposure, and future provider-specific webhook auth.
Emit only a sanitized schema-encoded preflight result and no secret values.
mandatoryVerification: Read-only preflight cannot deploy, alias, write env vars, provision storage, or mutate profiles.
Flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn operations; expected errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical schemas/types/errors and Config.redacted; no unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, raw JSON serialization, process.env reads, or helper sprawl.
Tests cover correct personal scope and each wrong-target/shared-config/missing-auth/stale-URL rejection.
Eve route-auth tests cover valid Vercel OIDC, invalid/missing bearer in deployed mode, and localhost-only development fallback.
Focused app checks and bun run verification pass.
completionCriteria: A documented command returns a sanitized go/no-go result for Production.
The auth/config matrix and stable proxy URL contract are recorded in app docs.
All three audit passes are recorded.

tasks.title: Provision isolated Production storage, credentials, and Codex profile
tasks.description: After explicit operator approval, create the separate Production storage/profile namespace, internal bearer, cipher key/key id, subject, and trusted-local OAuth profile, then prove ciphertext-only readback.
implementationNotes: Confirm Production Upstash target bindings and namespace without values; do not assume Marketplace automatic binding is correctly isolated.
Generate independent Production credentials. Never reuse Preview internal token, routing/profile prefix, cipher key, or lock/fence records.
Run only the package-owned trusted-local OAuth/profile provision command. Vercel receives the encrypted profile but no browser, callback, PKCE, code, or plaintext token operation.
Read back schema-decoded sanitized metadata and ciphertext/plaintext-marker checks; do not print the envelope or identifiers that are secret.
Record recovery inputs and current profile generation/revision without creating a downgrade path.
mandatoryVerification: Explicit approval is recorded before any Production mutation.
Flat named Effect programs and package-owned profile/cipher/store services; tagged errors handled in .pipe(...).
Canonical OAuth schemas and Config.redacted; no casts, DTO mirrors, manual mappers/readers, JSON.stringify/JSON.parse, token inspection, or wrapper sprawl.
Tests/proofs cover target separation, encrypted write/read, absent plaintext markers, refresh readiness, lock/fence namespace, and sanitized failures.
@bundjil/codex-oauth checks and bun run verification pass.
completionCriteria: Production has a separate encrypted, refresh-capable profile and credentials in the personal Vercel scope.
No Preview profile/cipher/lock/fence key is shared.
All three audit passes are recorded.

tasks.title: Deploy and verify the Production Codex proxy
tasks.description: Deploy apps/codex-proxy to Production, verify the immutable deployment, accept its stable Production alias, and record private live-provider proof before the agent references it.
implementationNotes: Deploy from the accepted source SHA using only encrypted Production variables and target bindings.
Prove live mode, stable alias ownership, Deployment Protection/private bearer behavior, health, one authenticated SSE completion, encrypted profile access, refresh/fencing readiness, and clean logs.
Reject any mock/local/API-key/Gateway fallback and any Preview URL or Tilt Legal project ownership.
Record current and previous accepted immutable deployments plus environment configuration version for rollback.
mandatoryVerification: No agent Production env or deployment changes until this task passes.
Flat Effect proof/config flows, typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag/catchTags/mapError, canonical schemas, and no unsafe casts/DTO mirrors/manual readers/raw JSON/helper sprawl.
Direct health, missing/invalid bearer 401, authenticated SSE 200/content type, refresh/storage, log, and leak checks pass.
Production stable URL resolves to the accepted immutable deployment.
Proxy/Codex OAuth checks and bun run verification pass.
completionCriteria: A stable personal-scope Production proxy URL is accepted and privately authenticated.
Rollback deployment/config references are recorded.
All three audit passes are recorded.

tasks.title: Deploy and verify the Production Eve agent through Codex
tasks.description: Configure the agent Production target with the stable Production proxy URL and separate bearer, deploy it, and prove one protected minimal Eve session through the Production proxy.
implementationNotes: Set only encrypted Production variables; do not point at an immutable Preview URL or inject build values from the CLI.
Inspect the build for the scoped Turbo environment contract and confirm the Eve manifest reports the intended Codex proxy model/context window.
Use fresh Vercel OIDC auth to inspect info, create a minimal non-sensitive session, replay startIndex=0 to a terminal waiting/completed state, and correlate the proxy request.
Observe for the defined soak window before enabling Sendblue or another external channel.
mandatoryVerification: Eve vercelOidc() and Deployment Protection remain active; no anonymous default Eve route is introduced.
Flat Effect config/proof flows, tagged pipe error handling, canonical schemas, no casts/DTO mirrors/manual readers/raw JSON/trivial wrappers.
Info/model/context-window, session stream, proxy correlation, log, and leak checks pass without Gateway/mock/Preview fallback.
Agent/proxy targeted checks and bun run verification pass.
completionCriteria: One Production Eve session reaches the verified stable Production proxy and Codex provider.
Source SHA, agent/proxy deployments, config versions, and sanitized evidence correlate.
All three audit passes are recorded.

tasks.title: Implement monitoring and execute a rollback drill
tasks.description: Add sanitized production health/auth/provider/storage/refresh signals and prove the ordered agent/config/proxy rollback procedure without rolling profile generations backward.
implementationNotes: Monitor deployment/source identity, health and completion latency/status, Eve terminal events, OAuth expiry/refresh/401 replay, lock/fence outcomes, storage errors, and auth rejection rates.
Exclude prompts, outputs, headers, tokens, raw profiles/ciphertext, PII, and bypass-bearing URLs from logs/alerts.

Drill rollback against controlled deployments/configuration: stop external traffic, restore the agent, restore matching env and redeploy, restore proxy, preserve newest fenced profile generation, then rerun proof.
Document credential rotation and re-login paths separately from deployment rollback.
mandatoryVerification: Flat Effect monitoring/proof operations with canonical schemas, tagged pipe errors, and Config.redacted.
No unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, raw JSON, broad lint suppression, or generic monitoring helpers.
Synthetic failure tests cover health, auth spike, storage failure, refresh/reauth, wrong mode/URL/scope, and leak detection.
Rollback drill ends on an accepted deployment/configuration with a passing minimal session and no profile generation downgrade.
bun run verification and git diff --check pass.
completionCriteria: Alerts and a no-content/no-secret dashboard or log query are documented and verified.
The rollback runbook has exercised evidence, not only prose.
All three audit passes are recorded.

tasks.title: Reconcile production docs, ledgers, and final verification
tasks.description: Update every current architecture/app/SPEC status statement to the actual Production implementation and evidence, then run the final repository gate.
implementationNotes: Update README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, apps/agent/README.md, apps/codex-proxy/README.md, docs/architecture/eve-agent.md, related Codex SPEC/task ledgers, and the active execution plan.
Remove stale claims that combined hosted Eve-to-live-proxy proof or Production are unverified only when the corresponding evidence exists.
Document Production auth, stable URL, profile provisioning/re-login, monitoring, rollback, and explicit Sendblue gating without secret values.
Run stale-language, Preview URL, wrong-scope, mock/local fallback, raw serialization, secret marker, and task-ledger consistency scans.
mandatoryVerification: Docs match the implemented production/test/CLI call graphs and package/app ownership.
Documentation examples require canonical Effect schemas/config and do not introduce DTO mirrors, manual readers, raw JSON, or helper sprawl.
Any reconciliation script or code change keeps flat meaningful Effect flows, tagged errors handled in .pipe(...), canonical Schema-derived types, and no unsafe casts or trivial wrappers.
All targeted checks, bun run verification, git diff --check, and task-ledger evidence checks pass.
No Production credential, full protected URL, prompt/output, phone/email, profile record, or ciphertext is committed.
completionCriteria: Current docs and ledgers state exactly what is live, what was proved, and what remains gated.
Every preceding task has three or more accepted audit passes and complete sanitized evidence.
Repository verification is green.
